message:
fix(combinator): circuit breaker CallGuard prevents probe permit leak on panic

Previously, if the user-supplied operation panicked inside `call()`, the
half-open probe permit was never returned — the probe slot stayed occupied
forever, eventually causing all subsequent calls to be rejected with
HalfOpenFull even though no real probes were active.

The fix introduces a `CallGuard` RAII wrapper that holds the permit during
the operation.  If the closure panics (or an early return bypasses the
normal success/failure path), the guard's Drop impl calls `record_failure`
so the circuit transitions back to Open and the probe slot is freed.

Also clamps `half_open_max_probes` to `MAX_HALF_OPEN_PROBES` in the
constructor to prevent silent misconfigurations.

Updates the `call_panics_leak_probes` regression test from documenting
the known bug to asserting the fix: panic now records a failure and
reopens the circuit, and subsequent calls get `Open` rather than
`HalfOpenFull`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
